claimText: ISO 20022 is an open global standard for financial information messaging adopted by Indian banks.
evidenceExcerpt: The ISO 20022 is an open global standard for financial information.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the purpose of ISO 20022 in banking.
distractorHooks: Confusing ISO 20022 with a domestic payment gateway like UPI
Describing it as a proprietary closed network instead of an open global standard

claimText: SWIFT is a secure messaging network headquartered in Belgium that facilitates global financial transactions.
evidenceExcerpt: It is a secure messaging network facilitating global financial transactions between over 11,000 institutions across 200 countries. Headquarters: Belgium.
examUse: Statement-based question on the function and headquarters of SWIFT.
distractorHooks: Claiming SWIFT is a clearing house or central bank rather than a messaging network
Stating SWIFT is headquartered in the USA or Switzerland

claimText: BRICS Pay is a proposed cross-border payment system aimed at reducing the dependence of BRICS member nations on the SWIFT messaging network.
evidenceExcerpt: BRICS Pay is a proposed cross-border payment system to facilitate trade and financial transactions among BRICS member to reduce dependence on SWIFT.
examUse: Statement-based question on BRICS initiatives and alternatives to the SWIFT system.
distractorHooks: Claiming BRICS Pay is an initiative by the G7 or World Bank
Stating it is a domestic currency replacement rather than a cross-border payment system
